ui.hungryTitle: The party is starving — health drops 5%/day
ui.restartConfirmText: Your current journey will be lost and the game will begin anew.

transport.eaglesLeftText: The eagles tired of carrying you and returned to the skies.
transport.eaglesNeedGandalf: The eagles cry: "We care for none but Gandalf."
transport.disembarkAsk: Step ashore? The ship will be left behind.

location.exploreFound: You searched the surroundings and found a hidden item!
location.exploreEmpty: You searched the surroundings but found nothing.
location.weathertopRune: Atop Weathertop you find a flat stone scratched with signs: a rune 'G' and three strokes beneath it — Gandalf's mark. He was here on the third. Press on east, to Rivendell.
location.erechSummon: At the Stone of Erech, Aragorn, heir of Isildur, summons the Oathbreakers — the Dead of Dunharrow who once swore to Isildur and broke their oath. He bids them fulfil it at last. The King of the Dead bows and agrees to follow until the oath is fulfilled. From now on the undead dare not assail your company.
location.corsairPeace: The Corsair captain hears you out and, judging your company wise, strikes a bargain: the corsairs will not trouble you at sea from now on.

denethor.pyre: Steward Denethor did not wait for you. In despair he mounted the pyre and gave himself to the fire in the House of the Stewards. A shadow lies over Minas Tirith, and the city keeps its mournful silence. May he rest — proud he was, yet he served Gondor faithfully to the very end.

recruit.celebornFollows: Where my lady Galadriel goes, I go.
recruit.haldirFollows: If the Lady of Lórien wills it, we come.

character.strengthHint: Attack damage and half your health (×5).
character.defenseHint: Cuts incoming damage and gives half your health (×5).
character.intelligenceHint: Crit frequency and smart target choice; a clever party (high average) meets fewer foes.
character.luckHint: Chance to hit and dodge, fleeing a fight, foraging and search finds.

orodruin.title: Orodruin
orodruin.text: You stand at the brink of Mount Doom. The Ring burns your hand. Choose.
orodruin.claim: Claim it and become the Lord
orodruin.noRing: You stand at the brink of Mount Doom, but the Ring is not with you — there is nothing to do here.

battle.title: Battle
battle.skipTitle: Jump straight to the battle result
battle.ringTitle: The Ring hides the bearer — most blows miss, but +1 day of corruption
battle.phialNote: Shelob recoils from the Phial's light — her strength is halved.
battle.ringNote: These foes see through the Ring — invisibility won't save you.
battle.balrogNote: The Balrog is mighty — ordinary blows barely scratch it. Only Gandalf, Bombadil or Saruman strike hard.
battle.betrayalNote: Nowhere to run — fight for the Ring.
battle.rogueNote: The Ring keeps him all but invisible — only a quarter of blows land.
battle.guardHint: Shield this hero — every second blow aimed at him is taken by another
battle.focusHint: Rally the whole party onto this foe
battle.focused: The party is ganging up on him

encounter.title: You meet an enemy
encounter.dangerous: ⚠ Strong — better flee and return later

escape.failedText: The enemy was too quick — there was no getting away.

death.title: {{names}}
death.text_one: Supplies ran out, and {{names}} did not survive the hunger. The party travels on.
death.text_other: Supplies ran out, and {{names}} did not survive the hunger. The party travels on without them.
death.battleText_one: {{names}} fell in battle. The party travels on.
death.battleText_other: {{names}} fell in battle. The party travels on without them.

ending.starvedText: Supplies ran out, and {{name}} did not survive the hunger. The quest is over.
ending.battleText: {{name}} fell in battle, and none of the company is left alive. The Ring is lost; the quest is over.
ending.victoryText: Mount Doom swallowed the Ring. Sauron is undone, Middle-earth is saved.
ending.gollumFallTitle: The Ring goes to the Fire
ending.gollumFallText: No sooner had {{name}} set the Ring upon a finger than Gollum sprang from the dark, mad with longing for the Precious. They wrestled together upon the very brink — and then, with a last shriek of "Precious!", he toppled over the edge into the Crack of Doom, the Ring still clutched to him. The Fire took them both. So, beyond all hope, the Ring was unmade: Sauron is undone, and Middle-earth is saved.
ending.claimText: The Ring prevailed. {{name}} seized power. Game over.
ending.betrayTitle: {{name}} could not let the Ring go
ending.betrayText: You chose to destroy the Ring, but at the last moment {{name}} slipped it onto a finger and declared himself the new Lord. Game over.
ending.lordText: Darkness took the bearer. {{name}} proclaims himself the new Lord. Game over.
ending.nothingText: {{name}} bested you and vanished with the Ring. No one is left to give chase. The quest ends with nothing.
ending.sauronText: Sauron could not believe his own Eye: the Ring had come to him of its own accord. It slipped from {{name}}'s finger, leapt into its master's hand — and so ended everything for Middle-earth.
ending.sarumanLordText: There is no one left to bear the Ring. Saruman takes it for himself and proclaims himself the new Lord. Game over.
ending.boromirGondorText: Boromir carries the Ring to Gondor, to Denethor. But Sauron falls upon Minas Tirith, breaks the city, and seizes the Ring. The quest is over.
ending.bombadilLostText: Tom Bombadil takes the Ring lightly — it holds no power over him. But he has no use for it either, and in time he simply loses it.
ending.gollumHidesText: Gollum snatches his Precious and vanishes with it into the wild forever. The Ring is lost to all.
ending.treebeardBuriesText: Treebeard will not bear the Ring to the fire, nor keep it — he buries it deep in Fangorn. The quest is over.
ending.deadKeepText: The Dead have no use for the Ring. It is lost among the shadows of Dunharrow, and its trail fades forever.
ending.rogueLordText: You were too late. {{name}} reached Mount Doom first and proclaimed himself the new Lord. Game over.
ending.valinorAsk: The ship has reached the edge of the world. Attempt to reach Valinor?
ending.valinorAskEagle: The Eagles have borne you to the edge of the world above the Sea. Ask them to fly the Straight Road into Valinor?
ending.valinorReturn: The Straight Road did not open, but the sea spared you — the ship comes out of the Shadowy Seas back to the shores of Middle-earth.
ending.valinorEagleRefuse: The Eagles will not bear the Ring into the West — the Valar would not receive it. While it endures they wheel about and carry you back to the shores of Middle-earth.
ending.valinorWestText: The Ring is unmade, and as a reward to the Bearers the Ban relents — the white ship takes the Straight Road, the grey rain-curtain draws back, and there before you rise white shores and a green country under a swift sunrise.
ending.valinorRingTitle: The Ring sinks into the ocean
ending.valinorRingText: The Valar will not suffer the Ring at their shores: the Straight Road stays shut, the ship founders in the Shadowy Seas, and the Ring sinks into the ocean — not destroyed, but beyond reach for an age.
ending.valinorSinkText: Without the grace of the Valar the Straight Road is not open to mortals — the ship wanders the Shadowy Seas and on the seventh day founders in a storm.

rogue.fledText: {{name}} could not resist the Ring and fled with it. His trail will take weeks to pick up — keep marching. You have two months before he reaches Mount Doom.

tharbad.gandalf: Desolation holds this place now. But the Greenway will open again, and between the Isen and the Greyflood, where the land ran wild, men and fields will return.
tharbad.boromir: The crossing at Tharbad... The ford over the Gwathló was so perilous that I lost my horse here and went on afoot.

sarumanParley.gandalf: Let us give him a chance to renounce his evil.
sarumanParley.treebeard: Enough! Hoom, hom… do not finish him. He is broken — look, no warrior now, but a shadow. Evil without power is not worth the killing.
sarumanParley.gimli: He must be killed, not let go. I do not believe in his mending!
sarumanParley.eomer: He drenched Rohan in blood. He must be put to death!
sarumanParley.prompt: What do you decide about Saruman?

partyTable.title: Party
partyTable.hint: Tap a hero to open their card

help.title: How to play
help.p1: First spread your points across Frodo's stats — or hit "Auto-play" to let the tale run itself. Then lead the Ring-bearer across Middle-earth to Mount Doom: click the map to travel, drag to pan, scroll to zoom. Red dots are places — stop there to recruit companions, restock food, search for hidden items, or take transport (pony, horse, ship, and — with Gandalf along — the Eagles). Mountains can be crossed but slow you to a crawl.
help.p2: Time passes in days, and the party eats 1 food a day — restock in towns or forage on the road. Switch on "Healing" on the supply bar to mend wounds for 2 extra food a day. With no food left the company starves, losing health each day, and at zero a member dies.
help.p3: Battles strike on the road. "Fight" plays out as an auto-battle; "Flee" gets you away but you drop nearly all your supplies. Mid-fight the bearer can "Put on the Ring" to vanish from almost every blow — but each time costs a day of corruption, and a few foes (the Ringwraiths) see right through it. Elven cloaks from Lórien help you slip past ambushes.
help.p4: Each hero has stats, an ability, and a level that grows from victories (spend the points it earns). The Ring's hold on its bearer deepens over time; you can pass it to a steadier companion with "Make bearer". Let it reach 100% and he bolts with it toward Mount Doom — hunt him down before it's lost for good. Mind your company, too: some won't march together (elves and a dwarf, say), and a few may turn on the bearer. Reach Orodruin and choose: cast the Ring into the Fire (victory) or claim it for yourself (darkness).

itemLike: Ha! Now this is a fine piece — stand back!
With this I could dance and fight at once!
itemDislike: Thanks and all… but my heart's not in it.
itemOff: Off it comes — my back's not made of iron.
ringTake: This shiny thing? Fine, I'll carry it…
ringGive: Whew, take it! Gives me the shivers.
Here, I rest easier without it.
food: My belly's about to shrink to a pea!
transport: A pony! No more trudging on my poor feet!
eagles: I'm flyiiing! Just don't look down… oh.
An eagle! Main thing — don't fall off.
levelStr: Feeling strong — I'd wrestle a troll now!
I feel a surge of strength!
Whoa, I'm a powerhouse now!
My muscles are buzzing!
Who's the strong one? Me!
levelDef: Now you couldn't dent me with an anvil!
Tough as a nut, that's me!
Go on, hit me — doesn't hurt!
My hide's like leather now!
You won't knock me down easy!
levelInt: I think I got smarter! …or did I?
I got smart — scares me a bit!
levelLuck: I feel lucky! I'd bet a button on it.
Born under a lucky star, I was!
bearer60: It's gone heavy. And it whispers. I hate the whispering.
bearer80: I don't want to give it up. It's mine!… oh, what am I saying.
idle: Did no one bring the dice?
I'd give anything for some pipe-weed…
Why are we standing? Moss is growing on my boots.
mapChange: Ooh, this is my favourite map!
terrainOn: Now I can finally see everything!
mountains: How long over these mountains? My legs are falling off!
When do these rocks ever end, eh?
sea: I think I'm going to be seasick… bleh.
Always dreamed of sailing! …maybe I was wrong.
hurt: Ow… I could use a rest, I'm all battered.
I think I've had enough — need a breather!
battleEasy: That's it? I was just warming up!
battleHard: What a brawl — nearly knocked the wind out of me!
What a mess, my bones are humming.
A bit more and we'd have been done for.
battleNarrow: Got out by a miracle! I thought we were done for.
A hair's breadth more and that'd be it!
Barely got my legs out of there!

itemLike: Good gear, it'll serve in a fight.
Just what I needed. My thanks.
itemOff: I'll take it off for now.
I'll set it aside a while.
ringTake: I'll carry it. As long as I can.
Trust it to me — I won't fail.
ringGive: Guard it well. It's no trinket.
Take it. It's surer this way.
food: We need to restock our supplies.
transport: We'll go faster mounted. We'll take it.
A good choice — it saves our strength.
eagles: With the eagles we'll get there fast.
levelStr: I'm stronger now. My arm is steady.
I feel a surge of strength.
More force in my blows.
I've grown tougher.
Strength is rising in me.
levelDef: I'm hardier now, I'll hold out longer.
Harder to wound me now.
I'm sturdier than before.
I'll take more blows.
I stand firm.
levelInt: I'm sharper now — I see more clearly.
bearer60: The Ring weighs on me, heavier with every step.
bearer80: I feel ill. The Ring scarcely lets me go.
idle: We shouldn't dawdle. Shall we move?
A long halt does us no good.
mapChange: A new map. We'll get used to it.
terrainOn: The lay of the land reads clearer now.
speedDown: Let's slow down — surer that way.
mountains: These mountains wear me down. Much further?
sea: The sea is new to me.
hurt: I'm badly hurt. I need rest.
I must rest and tend my wounds.
battleHard: That was hard going. Let's catch our breath.
Everyone took a beating — we'll tend the wounds.
battleNarrow: We barely survived. A close call.
We were a hair from death.
We barely pulled that one out.

itemLike: A worthy gift. I shall not shame it.
A gift that has known noble hands.
itemDislike: This is not my burden to bear.
Leave it to one it will serve better.
itemOff: I'll set it by until the hour of need.
I'll lay it aside for now.
ringTake: I take this burden, and may my strength suffice.
I'll bear it, while my will holds.
ringGive: Take it — and may it not break you.
Receive it. I have borne enough.
food: Without provisions we'll go no further.
Hunger is a worse foe than orcs.
transport: A good steed is half the journey.
The sea will bear us. Raise the sail.
eagles: All Endórë below us — great wings carry us on.
The Eagles of Manwë! A high honour.
levelStr: New might courses through my limbs.
I feel a surge of strength.
The strength of old wakes in me.
My arm grows mighty.
Power flows through my veins.
levelDef: No blade nor arrow shall break me now.
I am firm as the mountain stone.
Wounds yield before my resolve.
I cannot be broken.
My shield is my very will.
levelInt: My mind is keener than ever.
The wisdom of years descends on me.
My thought runs deep as the Sea.
levelLuck: The stars shine on me now.
Fortune weaves me a fair thread.
A good omen is upon me.
bearer60: The burden sears my breast. I hear the call of the Dark.
bearer80: The light dims. I hold on by my last strength…
idle: The Enemy does not sleep — we must hasten.
Time slips away like water through the fingers.
mapChange: Other lands lie open before us.
terrainOn: Now the very shape of the land is bared.
mountains: The mountains test us. Yet we shall endure.
Stone and steep — a stern road.
sea: The great sea bears us on — wondrous and dread.
The waters of Belegaer beneath the keel — who'd have thought.
mourn: {{name}} has fallen. May the light receive them.
hurt: My wounds are grievous. Grant me respite.
I hold on, but I need rest.
battleEasy: The foe was weak. We barely broke a sweat.
We swept through the foe like a storm.
battleHard: The fray was fierce. Yet we held.
battleNarrow: Only by a miracle did we live through that.
Death breathed on us — yet we live.
We looked death in the eye.

ringTake: It comes back to us, yesss!
ringGive: Noo! Give it back!… kheh, fine.
My… my precious… they take it, nasty!
itemLike: Shiny, good thing, we likes it!
Sss, it'll do! We keeps it, precious.
itemDislike: It burns us! No, no, we hates it!
itemOff: Off, off, we don't wants it!
food: Hungry, we're so hungry… a nice juicy fish!
transport: We don't likes horses, no! Pad-pad is better, sss.
eagles: High up! Scary, scary! Wicked birds!
idle: What are we waiting for, eh? Gollum, gollum.
Master dawdles… and we're tired of waiting, sss.
bearer60: Preciousss… heavy, but ours, isn't it?
bearer80: Mine! We won't give it up! The precious is ourss!
levelStr: Gollum's stronger now, sss!
Now we grips and grabs harder!
levelDef: Harder to catch us now, yes-yes!
Our skin's tougher, sss!
levelLuck: The fish swims right to us, sss!

itemLike: Hoom-hom… a good thing. We'll not be hasty.
itemDislike: Burárum… not for an old Ent, this.
itemOff: I'll set it down. Haste is not our way.
ringTake: Hoom… a heavy seed. I'll bear it, but do not hurry me.
ringGive: Take it. Such things sit ill with old branches.
food: Root and water nourish me… but little folk need victuals, hrum.
transport: I'll not ride. Ents go on their own — slow and sure.
eagles: Hoom-hoom… the wind is high. Strange to old boughs.
idle: Do not be hasty… yet we should not take root here. Hrum.
bearer60: Hoom… it whispers even through bark. Not good.
bearer80: Burárum… the dark reaches for the roots. Heavy going.
levelStr: Hoom-hom… my roots grow strong.
The old tree still brims with might.
levelDef: My bark is thicker than stone, burárum.
Neither axe nor storm shall fell me.
levelInt: Hoom… long thoughts ripen in me.
The memory of the earth gathers in my rings.
levelLuck: Hoom-hoom… the wind favours the boughs.
A good season settles on the wood.

itemLike: Cold and familiar… I will take it.
itemDislike: The dead have no need of living men's gifts.
itemOff: Dust to dust. I leave it.
food: The dead know no hunger… but it is dread to the living.
transport: I follow you. The road means nothing to me.
eagles: Even death rises into the sky now.
idle: Time holds no sway over the dead. But the oath waits to be kept.
bearer60: The Ring… it calls to us, the fallen, too. Beware.
bearer80: The dark knows its own shadow. Hasten, living one.
levelStr: A mortal might returns to me.
The hand of the dead is strong again.
levelDef: I cannot be slain twice.
Steel passes through shadow in vain.
levelInt: I remember all that was before death.
Ages of silence have honed my mind.
levelLuck: The lot of the dead is already cast.
Fate holds no sway over the fallen.

split.title: Split the party
split.hint: Leave a companion here (walk back and call them later) or dismiss them for good.
split.empty: There is no one in the party besides the bearer.

samCatchUp.title: Sam Catches Up
samCatchUp.text: "Master Frodo, you didn't think you'd go alone and I'd leave you, did you?"

refuse.gollumHobbits: Gollum hisses: "No! Gollum goes only with hobbitses, no strangers, gollum!"
refuse.grima: I'll stay by the king — someone must guard his peace from the likes of you.
refuse.theodenGrima: Wormtongue whispers in the king's ear: "What need have you, lord, of these wanderers and their mad errand?" — and Théoden turns away.
refuse.grimaFlees: Wormtongue paled at the sight of Gandalf and slipped away into the gloom of the hall — and was never seen in Edoras again.
refuse.elrondArwen: My father will not let me go.
refuse.kinEowyn: My kin will not let me go.
refuse.sarumanGandalf: Saruman: "I will not walk beside that grey vagabond."
refuse.gandalfSaruman: Gandalf: "I will not stand beside the traitor Saruman."
refuse.elfDwarf: I'll not take a step while a dwarf is in the company.
refuse.cirdanWisdom: Cirdan: "I will put to sea only with a company wiser, on the whole, than I."
refuse.bilbo: Bilbo grumbles: "No, no — I'm not so young anymore. Adventures aren't for me."
refuse.denethor: Denethor: "I have no time for mad schemes. The Ring belongs to Minas Tirith."
refuse.denethorFree: Denethor: "I have no time for such foolishness."
moodLines: "Not today — I have things to do."
"Not in the mood for long roads."
refuse.gollumFled: Gollum hisses and slinks off into the dark.
refuse.traitorReturns: {{name}} comes to his senses and leaves the party in shame.
refuse.bombadilLeaves: Tom Bombadil misses his home and leaves the party.
refuse.eomerSendsEowyn: Éomer: "Sister, ride back to Edoras." Éowyn heads home.
refuse.arwenLeaves: My father does not permit it — I must leave the company.
refuse.treebeardStays: Hoom, hom… go, my friends. I will not come. I will stay, and grow again what Saruman cut down.

chronicle.empty: The chronicle is still empty — the road has only just begun.

talk.cloaksDesc: for the whole party — fewer random encounters
talk.hobbit4: Hullo there! Fancy a bite to eat?
talk.serious2: Glad to see you here in {{place}}.
talk.serious4: Well met. How may I help?
talk.lofty1: I greet you, wanderer. May your road be bright.
talk.lofty2: May the light of the Valar keep you.
talk.lofty3: Welcome to {{place}}. May the grace of the Valar be with you.

ability.king_dead: Heals for 30–60% of damage dealt to the living
ability.haldir: Bonus damage versus orcs and goblins
ability.galdor: A ship is always in port
ability.gandalf_white: The whole company gains 25% more battle experience
ability.faramir: Tightens the shield wall: only a quarter of blows reach a guarded hero
ability.bilbo: Whole party's luck +25% of the party's average luck

gate.question: Say “friend” in Sindarin, and enter.
gate.hint: The Doors of Moria open on a single word.
gate.wrong: The gate does not stir. Try another word.

body: The goal: carry the Ring from Hobbiton to Mount Doom and destroy it in the fire of Orodruin. Along the way you can gather a company, find gear, and reach one of several endings.
Time passes in days — travelling, waiting, searching and foraging. Click the map to set a route; click a red dot to enter a location.
The portraits up top open a character: there you change the Ring-bearer and equip found items. The party can be split and reunited.
This guide is built straight from the game's data — stats, formulas and schedules always match the live code.

heroes.note: Base hero stats. HP = strength×5 + defense×5. “Resil.” is how many days a hero can bear the Ring before breaking.
heroes.legend: ⊘ — beyond the Ring's hold; will never bear it.

enemies.note: Foes by rising strength. HP = strength×5 + defense×5. XP is the reward for beating them.
enemies.regionsLegend: Regions: NW Eriador · NE Mirkwood · MW Rohan · ME Mordor/Gondor · SW/SE the far south.
enemies.bossesNote: Location guardians — powerful foes barring key places. Some can only be passed under special conditions.

items.note: Special items: found by exploring locations or given as gifts. Bonuses apply while the item is worn.

recruit.scheduleNote: These appear at different places on set dates (day 0 = 23 September 3018).
recruit.alwaysNote: Found in their own lands at any time (sometimes gated by a luck check).
recruit.roamingNote: These aren't found in one place — they roam their lands and turn up on the road (sometimes after a fight).

roaming.gollum: Skulks after the party — met at random on the road; at Mount Doom he may snatch the Ring.
roaming.eomer: Patrols Rohan — met in a skirmish with orcs, especially with Rohirrim along.
roaming.treebeard: Wanders Fangorn — met at random by the forest near Isengard.
roaming.gandalf_white: Returns after the Grey Gandalf falls in battle — met on the road about a month later.

conditions: Bombadil and Grimbeorn aren't always home: Bombadil turns up on about 1 visit in 5, Grimbeorn 1 in 2.
Bilbo and Denethor are reluctant — they only relent after several attempts to persuade them.
Círdan joins only if the party is, on the whole, wiser than he is.

Saruman is recruited at Isengard only if the bearer is duller than him and no Gandalf or Treebeard is along; otherwise he's a boss. Spare him and you loose him to roam the north-west — two months on he comes to scour the Shire.
Gríma sits at Edoras until Gandalf drives him out; then he flees to Saruman or roams the wild until he is slain.
Treebeard, brought to fallen Isengard, may settle to rule it — then he leaves the party and is found there ever after.
On 15 March 3019 the despairing Steward Denethor gives himself to the fire — after that he can no longer be recruited at Minas Tirith.

locations.note: 33 places of Middle-earth. Click a picture to cycle the season.

combat.ringF: the bearer is all but invisible — only ~{{pct}} of blows land
combat.guard: Click your hero — the party shields him: only {{pass}} of blows reach him, the rest are taken by others (with Faramir along, just {{faramir}}). A bearer wearing the Ring can't be shielded. Click again to lift.
combat.focusOrder: Click a foe — the party gangs up on it, overriding wit-based target choice. Click again to lift.
combat.statsNote: Strength — damage and HP. Defense — HP and less damage (but at least 34% of an attack always lands). Intelligence — crits, focusing the dangerous foe, caution. Luck — hitting, dodging, fleeing and searching.

levels.note: Leaving a level costs level×{{base}} XP. Each level past the first grants 1 stat point. Leveling is uncapped — the table just shows the first few.
levels.pointNote: You start with Frodo alone; the rest are recruited on the road.
levels.enemyNote: Over time every foe (bosses included) gains +1 to a random stat — as if the enemy ranks level up as well. The interval grows: 10 days to the 1st point, then 11, 12, and so on, so enemies decelerate just like your own leveling.
levels.enemyFoot: Points scatter at random over strength/defense/intelligence/luck (strength and defense also raise HP). Pack sizes already scale to your party. Rough guide: ~11 points by six months, ~19 by a year.

secrets.note: Non-obvious mechanics read straight from the code: how to unlock and try everything the game holds.
